canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, Mean Opinion Score (MOS) is consistently framed as a subjective evaluation metric that relies on human raters. Its application, however, varies by modality. One paper defines and uses MOS to assess the quality of synthesized speech, where human listeners rate attributes such as "naturalness" on a 1–5 scale. In contrast, another study applies MOS to evaluate text, using a "Likert-like scale" for human judgments on "text quality" and "diversity." In the context of text evaluation, the qualities it is said to capture include perceived fluency and coherence. Therefore, while the core mechanism of human-rated scoring is shared, the specific phenomena measured by MOS—speech naturalness versus text quality and diversity—differ depending on the research context.
papers: Judging Quality Across Languages: A Multilingual Approach to Pretraining Data Filtering with Language Models
TACO: Enhancing Multimodal In-context Learning via Task Mapping-Guided Sequence Configuration
VisBias: Measuring Explicit and Implicit Social Biases in Vision Language Models

definitions.text: Mean Opinion Score, a subjective evaluation metric where human listeners rate the quality of synthesized speech (e.g., naturalness) on a 1–5 scale.
definitions.paper: TACO: Enhancing Multimodal In-context Learning via Task Mapping-Guided Sequence Configuration

definitions.text: Human evaluation metric used to rate text quality and diversity on a Likert-like scale, capturing perceived fluency, coherence, and variation.
definitions.paper: Judging Quality Across Languages: A Multilingual Approach to Pretraining Data Filtering with Language Models
